name: USS Abraham Lincoln
area: Strait of Hormuz approaches — convoy escort operations
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
status: Engaged — convoy escort and strike operations
notes: Primary strike platform for Operation Epic Fury. Day 13: Provided air cover for first escorted tanker convoy through Hormuz. Moved closer to strait entrance. Air wing flying 220+ sorties/day including CAP over convoy route.

name: USS Gerald R. Ford
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II, EA-18G Growlers
status: Engaged — supporting IDF Lebanon ground operation and Iran strikes
notes: Day 13: Shifted closer to Lebanese coast to support IDF ground incursion into southern Lebanon. F-35Cs providing deep strike into western Iran. Dual mission intensifies as Lebanon front escalates to ground war.

name: USS George H.W. Bush
area: Gulf of Oman — southern convoy screening
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
status: Engaged — convoy screen and southern interdiction
notes: Day 13: Moved into Gulf of Oman to provide southern screening for Hormuz convoy operations. Covering approaches from the Arabian Sea. ASW helicopters active after unconfirmed submarine contact.

name: USS America
area: Gulf of Oman (near Strait of Hormuz) — mine-clearing ops
aircraft: F-35B Lightning II, MV-22 Osprey, AH-1Z Viper, CH-53E Super Stallion
status: Standing by — mine countermeasures support, TRAP
notes: Day 13: Directly involved in convoy escort through Hormuz. MH-53E mine countermeasures helicopters cleared lane ahead of tankers. AH-1Z Vipers drove off IRGC fast attack craft. Marines on heightened readiness.

name: USS Paul Ignatius
area: Strait of Hormuz approaches — escort operations
notes: Day 13: Lead escort for first tanker convoy through Hormuz. Intercepted 2 IRGC anti-ship missiles with SM-2 and CIWS. First direct US Navy-IRGC engagement. Ship undamaged. Aegis BMD capable.

name: USS Florida
area: Gulf of Oman (approx.) — repositioned closer to Hormuz
notes: Ohio-class guided missile submarine. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Day 13: Likely repositioned to support retaliatory strikes on IRGC coastal batteries after Hormuz convoy engagement. Exact position classified.

name: USS John Finn
notes: Detached from CSG-3 to Red Sea for Houthi missile defense and merchant shipping escort. Key node in southern blockade.
